dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 for a woman with abdominal pain at an address in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Dave, a paramedic. My partner is Lee. What made you call 911 today?
I have severe lower abdominal pain. It's been a week now, and it's worse today.
Ma'am, on a scale from 0 to 10, how would you rate your pain right now?
Ten. Definitely ten. It's like nothing I've ever felt before.
Can you tell me your full name, the date today, and where we are?
My name is Helen Loving. Today is the 14th of August, 2019. We're in [CITY].
Can you tell me the month and year again to confirm your orientation?
August 2019.
Airway is patent. She's not showing any signs of obstruction.
Breathing is unlabored, no use of accessory muscles. Rate and depth appear normal.
She's warm and pink. No cyanosis. Capillary refill is less than 2 seconds.
Heart rate is 88. EKG shows sinus rhythm. No dysrhythmias.
Respirations are 18, regular, no distress observed.
Blood pressure is 115/82. She's alert and oriented.
Pulse oximetry is 100%. Breathing comfortably on room air.
Blood glucose is 131. No signs of hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia.
When did your abdominal pain first start, and has it changed in intensity or location over time?

It started over a week ago in the lower abdominal area and is now nonstop, but suprapubic and more intense today. I'm nauseated, but I haven't vomited here yet. The only thing that helps is lying on the floor on a hard surface. I've had lots of diarrhea.
How have you been in the past with your health-do you have any medical problems that we should be aware of?
I have moderate asthma, controlled with long-acting meds. No other major issues after my checkup last year.
When was the last time you ate something?
I last ate about two days ago. I tried eating yesterday, but I threw up twice. I've been struggling with eating all week.
On a scale of 0 to 10, where would you place this pain compared to the worst you've had?
This is a 10/10. It's constant like something is hot in my belly and digging there.
Does the pain go anywhere else in your body, like your back or sides?
It stays right in the lower belly. Suprapubic, no other areas are affected.
I'm going to gently press here on your belly to check for tenderness or rigidity.
There is tenderness noted on suprapubic palpation. The abdomen is generally soft with no guarding or rebound tenderness.
We're going to run a four-lead ECG for monitoring. The reading shows sinus rhythm with no irregularity.
A 12-lead ECG is now being completed as part of our standard assessment for abdominal pain.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take Zoloft, Naltrexone, gabapentin, Voltaren, Tegretol-also progesterone, but I haven't taken them because of the stomach issues.
Given the findings and history, we're starting the abdominal pain protocol 3-2 and will transport you to [COMPANY_NAME] for further assessment.
Pulse is now 82-slightly decreased from initial reading. Continuing to monitor.
Respiratory rate remains at 18-still stable and no signs of distress.
Blood pressure is now 97/68. Watching this closely for any further drop.
Oxygen saturation remains at 100%. She's stable and breathing comfortably on her own.
Positioning her onto the stretcher now. Making sure to secure her with all the available straps.
She is transported in semi-Fowler's to keep her comfortable and preserve her airway.
Her blood pressure is still at 97/68-no change from the last check during transport.
No respiratory changes observed. Rate and depth are consistent and normal.
SpO2 is still 100%. She continues to tolerate transport well.
Did anything specific happen before the pain started, like a meal or physical activity?

I had some spicy food and exercised a bit more than usual a week ago. I was stressed too. I noticed something was off with the presence of bright red blood when I passed stool and had vaginal spotting.
Any nausea or vomiting while we've been here?
No, nothing new. No change in symptoms since you've arrived.
I thought maybe it would pass at home, but I couldn't move up from the bed.
How would you describe the feeling of the pain-sharp, dull, burning, or something else?
It feels like intense, hot pressure deep in the belly. It's not spreading and stays focused off my pubic region.
All documentation is complete, including initial vitals and ECGs. Her condition remains stable throughout transport.
Vitals recorded at two different time intervals. The 4-lead and 12-lead ECG results are available.
She was conscious and oriented with a GCS of 15 (Eye 4, Verbal 5, Motor 6). No complications noted during transport.
We are en route to [COMPANY_NAME]. The receiving facility is aware and has received our report.
She is secure and positioned in semi-Fowler's for transport. No new complaints or changes.
Your pain remains at a 10/10?
Yes, definitely 10. No change with position or time.
Vitals remain consistent with stable pulse, respiration, blood pressure and SpO2.
